description: Alolan Dugtrio enriched. Third distinct Alolan-form species in corpus (Grimer + Graveler + Dugtrio). Cohort cleanly clears the 3-species threshold the wave 23 commissioning anticipated. The LOT-123 print also adds a sixth designer-attested adaptation mechanism to the documented heterogeneity — cultural-religious folk-veneration per the canonical flavor text ('cherished in the Alola region, where they are thought to be feminine deities of the land incarnate') — alongside the prior five (climate, dietary divergence, predator-prey rebalancing, grief-ritual, institutional ecological introduction). The Yuka Morii clay-sculpted rendering style on this print is the Alola-folk-craft aesthetic register that thematically anchors the deity-veneration framing. The blueballs are mild: this is the 1:N fanout pattern, source-node-to-target-card, no card-to-card mirror. The cohort node is doing its job.
citations: [Pokemon Sun and Moon (Game Freak, 2016) primary canon]
[LOT-123 canonical flavor text: 'These Pokémon are cherished in the Alola region, where they are thought to be feminine deities of the land incarnate.']
files_modified: cards/_characters/alolan-regional-forms.md (appears_on appended LOT-123; body 'Cards in our corpus' section appended Alolan Dugtrio entry; opening line of cards-in-corpus updated to '4 cards across 3 distinct species'; Caveats updated to reflect 3-species threshold cleared; Per-species adaptation mechanisms paragraph extended to document six mechanisms via the LOT-123 cultural-religious folk-veneration addition)
other_candidates_considered: 1:1 LOT-123 ↔ CSE-130 Alolan Grimer mirror — refused. Cross-species mirrors within a cohort are redundant with the cohort node already anchoring both; the cohort-stack topology is structurally cleaner and serves the bundler better.
1:1 LOT-123 ↔ either of the two Alolan Graveler prints — refused, same reason.
